plans.dimensions: 54in long
materials: 1-1/4in pocket hole screws (or brad nails/self-tapping screws)
plans.extra: Part of Ana White's Pine and Plain free furniture plans collection
plans.original_description: A simple open-shelf console table using only pocket-hole joinery and no complex joints, from Ana White's Pine and Plain collection.
original_steps: Cut the top, two side, and bottom shelf pieces from 1x12 per the cut list.
Build the outer frame using 3/4in pocket holes, pocket screws, and glue.
Cut the three 1x4 support boards to 52-1/2in.
Attach the 1x4 supports with glue and nails/screws for added structure.
Fill any holes, sand, and finish with stain or paint.

plans.title: Raised Garden Bed with Trellis
plans.dimensions: About 5ft wide x 8ft long
construction_notes: Built with untreated pine deliberately, for a lower cost and to avoid treated-lumber chemicals near food - expect to replace in a few years
Exact board-length cut list not given in the free text - overall bed size and material list are as documented
plans.original_description: A raised garden bed with a decorative trellis built onto the back, sized at about 5ft x 8ft, built from standard untreated pine for a lower-cost, shorter-lived bed.
original_steps: Assemble the raised bed box from the 2x12 boards to about 5ft wide and 8ft long.
Reinforce the corners and sides with 2x4 framing.
Build the trellis frame from 2x2s and attach it to the back of the bed.
Weave or attach square dowels across the trellis frame in your chosen pattern (about 48ft of dowel used in the source build).
Fasten everything with exterior wood screws sized to the connection.

plans.title: X-Base Dining Table
plans.dimensions: Leg posts cut to 19-3/4in
construction_notes: Rough sawn cedar recommended for outdoor use (~$700 in materials); standard dimensional lumber works for indoor use (~$250)
Captures only the leg/stretcher joinery detail - the source's full board-by-board cut list continues further than what was extracted here
plans.original_description: An X-base dining table styled after Pottery Barn/Restoration Hardware designs, buildable in cedar for outdoor use or standard lumber for indoor use.
original_steps: Cut the leg posts from 4x4 stock to 19-3/4in and cut a mortise in the center of each for the stretcher tenon (or use structural screws only, skipping the joinery).
Build the X-shaped stretcher assembly connecting the leg posts.
Cut and assemble the table's apron/base frame from the remaining 4x4 and 2x4 stock.
Cut the tabletop boards from 2x10 stock and join edge-to-edge with biscuits, dowel pins, and exterior glue.
Attach the assembled top to the base with structural screws.

plans.title: Window Flower Box Planter
plans.dimensions: Sized to fit under the specific window - not itemized numerically
construction_notes: Exact board dimensions were not given in the source text - the box is sized to the individual window
plans.original_description: A pressure-treated wood flower box planter sized to fit under a window, built with pocket-hole joinery for curb appeal.
original_steps: Measure your window's width to determine the box's length.
Cut the pressure-treated boards for the front, back, sides, and bottom to fit.
Assemble with pocket holes, glue, and outdoor pocket screws.
Sand and paint the box.
Mount below the window with appropriate exterior screws or brackets.

plans.title: Large Sloped-Roof Dog House
plans.dimensions: 4ft wide x 4ft deep, sloped roof
cut_list: 2 - 2x2 @ 3ft 9in, 2 - 2x2 @ 3ft 9-5/8in (7.5-deg both ends), 2 - 2x2 @ 3ft 8-1/4in (7.5-deg one end), 2 - 2x2 @ 3ft 5-5/16in (7.5-deg one end), 2 - 2x2 @ 3ft 2-7/16in (7.5-deg one end) (side walls)
plans.extra: Source site also offers a free PDF download
plans.original_description: A large sloped-roof dog house measuring 4ft x 4ft, framed in 2x4/2x2 lumber and sided in T1-11 exterior panels with a shingled roof.
original_steps: Build the floor frame from 2x4s per the cut list and install a 3/4in plywood floor deck.
Build the front, back, and side wall frames from 2x2s per the cut list, angling the side-wall pieces 7.5 degrees where noted.
Assemble the wall frames onto the floor deck, nailing through the frame into the floor.
Cut and install the 2x2 roof ridge.
Cut and attach the T1-11 siding panels to the front, sides, and back.
Install the 1/2in plywood roof deck, then shingles, roofing felt, roof tacks/staples, and drip edge.
Add the 1x4 trim boards around the exterior.

plans.title: Demilune Table That Opens to a Full Circle
plans.dimensions: Half-round demilune that opens into a full circle via a sliding fourth leg and hinged top
plans.original_description: A half-round demilune console table that transforms into a full circular dining table - a fourth leg slides out from the back apron and the hinged top halves fold out to complete the circle.
original_steps: Cut and taper the four 2x2 legs to 29-3/4in.
Cut the arc-shaped front apron supports from MDF or plywood and attach to the legs with pocket holes.
Cut the front and back aprons, kerfing the front apron pieces so they curve along the arced supports.
Cut and hinge the two half-round top panels together.
Build the fourth-leg slide mechanism (aprons, slides, and stops) so a hidden leg extends from the back apron for support when the top opens.
Assemble the base to the top, edge-band exposed plywood edges, sand, and finish.

plans.title: Leaf-Shaped Nesting Tables
plans.dimensions: Template-based - three graduated leaf shapes sized to the builder's space
construction_notes: No numeric cut list - sizes and shapes are template-based and meant to be customized to the builder's space
plans.original_description: A set of three leaf-shaped nesting side tables cut from oak plywood using hand-drawn templates, on purchased furniture legs.
original_steps: Cut craft paper templates in a leaf shape, in three graduated sizes, sized to your own space.
Trace the templates onto oak plywood, orienting the grain to follow the leaf shape.
Cut out each tabletop with a scroll saw or jigsaw, then true up long edges with a circular saw.
Apply edge banding to all cut edges and trim flush.
Attach purchased furniture legs to the underside of each top with a Kreg jig and screws.
Sand and finish.

plans.title: Footed Charcuterie Board (Pieced 1x Boards)
plans.dimensions: Sized from a 1x3 @ 8ft board
plans.original_description: A footed charcuterie board pieced together from cheap 1x boards instead of one expensive slab, with a handle piece extending past the main boards.
original_steps: Cut the four 1x3 boards to 23-3/4in and lay them out edge to edge.
Cut the 1x2 handle piece to 28-1/2in, positioned so it extends past the other boards as a handle.
Glue the boards together edge to edge, including the handle piece.
Cut and glue the two 1x2 foot pieces to the underside to tie the boards together.
Round the corners with a jigsaw and sand thoroughly.
Finish with a food-safe butcher block finish.

plans.title: Inside-Cabinet Cutting Board Storage Rack
plans.dimensions: About 14.5in wide x 8in tall
plans.original_description: A slim inside-cabinet-door rack that holds cutting boards upright and organized instead of leaning loose against pots and pans.
original_steps: Cut all pieces to size per the cut list from a 1x4 board.
Glue and clamp two side pieces to the bottom piece, waiting about 30 minutes for the glue to set.
Glue the two back vertical pieces in place.
Optionally notch the side pieces to fit the back verticals for extra gluing surface and strength.
Glue on the bottom front and top front pieces to complete the rack.
Stain and seal with polyurethane, then mount inside a cabinet door.
